sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: TSG Hoffenheim, currently positioned 15th in the league with 31 points, face a formidable challenge as they host Borussia Dortmund, who sit in 5th place with an impressive 51 points. This Round 31 clash at the PreZero Arena in Sinsheim promises to be a test of resilience for the home side. With referee Benjamin Brand officiating, known for averaging 4.1 yellow cards per game, discipline will be crucial for both teams. Hoffenheim will be desperate to climb out of the lower half of the table, while Dortmund aim to solidify their top-tier standing.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Borussia Dortmund hold the upper hand with 3 wins compared to Hoffenheim’s single victory and 1 draw. Their most recent encounter on December 15, 2024, ended in a 1-1 stalemate, with Hoffenheim’s Alexander Prass earning Player of the Match honors with a 7.7 Sofascore rating. Historically, Dortmund have been first to score in 9 of the last 10 meetings and have won the first half in 5 of their last 6 encounters against Hoffenheim. Additionally, Hoffenheim have failed to keep a clean sheet against Dortmund in their last 10 head-to-head games.

sections.subtitle: Standout Players to Watch
sections.text: For Borussia Dortmund, Serhou Guirassy stands out as a major threat with 19 goals this season, ranking him 2nd in the league for goals scored. His recent form, with 5 goals in the last 5 matches and a 7.22 average Sofascore rating, makes him a focal point of Dortmund’s attack. Julian Brandt, another key player with a league rank of 4 for big chances created (15), has contributed 4 goals and 8 assists, alongside a consistent 7.07 average rating. Jamie Gittens also impresses with 2.6 successful dribbles per game, ranking 4th in the league. On the Hoffenheim side, Finn Ole Becker shines with a league-leading 77.8% aerial duels won, ranking 1st, while Andrej Kramarić, in fine form with 4 goals in his last 5 matches and an average rating of 7.38, leads the line with 11 goals and 8 assists.

sections.subtitle: Supporting Cast and Squad Depth
sections.text: Beyond the stars, Hoffenheim will rely on players like Adam Hlozek, who has outperformed his expected goals with 8 goals against an xG of 5.33, and Anton Stach, contributing 1 goal and 2 assists with a solid 7.11 average rating. Marius Bülter adds depth to the attack with 6 goals. For Dortmund, Karim Adeyemi offers pace and creativity with 6 goals and 5 assists, while Waldemar Anton provides defensive stability with 2 goals and 2 assists, boasting a strong 7.23 rating. These supporting players could tilt the balance in critical moments.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Borussia Dortmund enter this match with a streak of 3 games without a loss, though they’ve gone 10 games without a clean sheet, conceding in every match during this run. Their games have consistently seen more than 2.5 goals in the last 6 outings, with both teams scoring in 5 of those 6 matches. Hoffenheim, on the other hand, have had less than 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 matches, suggesting a more conservative or defensively vulnerable approach. The head-to-head trend of high corner counts, with over 10.5 corners in 5 of the last 6 meetings, hints at an open game with plenty of attacking intent.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights and Key Metrics
sections.text: Hoffenheim’s season stats reveal a mixed bag, with 44 goals scored (13th in the league) but a leaky defense conceding 62 goals (16th for least goals conceded). They excel in specific areas like accurate crosses, leading the league with 181, and penalty area entries per game, ranking 3rd with 32.06. Dortmund, meanwhile, show strength in late-game scoring, with 17 goals between the 61st and 75th minutes, accounting for 20.4% of their total. Hoffenheim’s disciplinary record is concerning, with 372 fouls committed (5th most in the league) and 64 yellow cards, which could be exploited by Dortmund’s technical players.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Tactical Impact
sections.text: Hoffenheim will miss midfielder Tom Bischof, who has 5 goals and 2 assists with a 7.28 average rating, potentially weakening their midfield creativity. Defender Valentin Gendrey’s absence further strains their backline. Dortmund, meanwhile, are without key forward Maximilian Beier (8 goals, 5 assists) and midfielder Pascal Groß, who ranks 4th in the league with 9 assists, which could dent their attacking fluidity. Defender Nico Schlotterbeck’s absence might also expose defensive gaps, despite Dortmund’s overall squad depth.
